label: The Sealed Alcove
description: The Sealed Alcove is a compact bedchamber hewn from salvaged ashlar and stitched into later brickwork, its low arch and smoke-darkened beams bearing the same repair seams that mark The Mortared Rooms. A narrow pallet recess occupies the thickest wall, set beneath a shuttered slit window whose iron pin-latch reflects the building’s defensive vernacular. Plaster patches and limewash are interrupted by a civic hazard placard fixed to a built-in niche, binding private rest to Varric Holdfast’s regime of posted interdictions. The floor combines mismatched flagstone with a strip of worn plank, kept serviceable and easily cleared in a structure raised over ruinfield foundations.
